corelogic_v10397           

For Boolean variables $p,q,r,s\in\{0,1\}$ let  

$$
G(p,q,r,s)=\Bigl[(p\to q)\equiv(r\oplus s)\Bigr]\land
            \Bigl[(q\to s)\lor(p\equiv\lnot r)\Bigr].
$$

Keep only those valuations satisfying  

1. $G(p,q,r,s)=1$,  
2. $p+q+r+s\ge 2$.

Write each surviving valuation as the $4$-bit integer  
$$
N=8p+4q+2r+s
$$
and compute  
$$
\Sigma=\sum\bigl(11N^{2}+5\bigr)\pmod{1009}.
$$

---

Filtering yields the integers  
$$
3,5,6,7,9,10,11,12,13,14,15.
$$
Hence  
$$
\Sigma\equiv\sum_{n}\!\bigl(11n^{2}+5\bigr)=652\pmod{1009}.
$$

652
